reason_summary.functional_viewpoint: Most people like being able to choose the time period themselves because it feels fun, simple, and gives them control over the experience. A smaller group also values this choice because it helps them follow the history they are most interested in.
reason_summary.dysfunctional_viewpoint: The majority of people would dislike this because they want to choose the time period themselves and have clear control over when their journey begins.

Feature: Engage with Historical Characters [Role: Regular User]

reason_summary.functional_viewpoint: Most people like meeting or interacting with historical figures because it makes the experience more fun, interesting, vivid, and memorable, and helps history feel clearer and more alive.
reason_summary.dysfunctional_viewpoint: Most people would be disappointed if they could not talk to or engage much with the historical characters, since that interaction is a major part of the fun and appeal. However, a smaller group would be willing to accept less interaction, though they would still miss the historical and conversational aspects.

Feature: Make Decisions that Affect the Story [Role: Regular User]

reason_summary.functional_viewpoint: The majority of people in this group enjoy making choices in a story, especially when those choices affect what happens and create branching paths or surprises. They want choices to matter and add depth, fun, and purpose to the experience, but they also prefer the options to be clear and not confusing or difficult.
reason_summary.dysfunctional_viewpoint: Most people would dislike a feature that makes their choices have no effect, because they want their decisions to matter and change the story or trip. A smaller group is okay with it if it makes the experience easier and less complicated.
